entry.title: HANA Cockpit retention not deleting old SYSTEMDB backup generations although log_mode is normal
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am testing HANA Cockpit backup retention for SYSTEMDB@HSD and would like to clarify if the behavior I am seeing is expected.

Configuration:

  • Backup destination: File
  • Backup path: /hana/shared/HSD/HDB00/backup/data/SYSTEMDB/
  • Daily complete data backup: around 02:00 AM
  • Automatically Delete Backup Generations: Yes
  • Deletion option: Remove from backup catalog and delete physically from file system
  • Retain Backup Generations Younger Than: 1 day
  • Minimum No. of Retained Backup Generations: 1
  • Start Daily Automatic Deletion: 9:00 PM UTC
  • Assigned Database User: SYSTEM
  • log_mode = normal

On the morning of Apr 28, I still saw these complete data backup generations in both the backup catalog and file system:

2026-04-26 02:00
2026-04-27 02:00
2026-04-28 02:00

However, when I checked again on Apr 29, the Apr 26 backup generation had been deleted, and the remaining backup files were:

2026-04-27 02:00
2026-04-28 02:00
2026-04-29 02:00

So the retention policy seems to be working, but not exactly as I initially expected.

My questions are:

  1. Is this behavior expected for Retain Backup Generations Younger Than = 1 day?
  2. Does HANA Cockpit retention evaluate and delete old backup generations only once per daily scheduler run, instead of deleting them immediately when they become older than 1 day?
  3. Why would the Apr 26 generation still exist on Apr 28 but be deleted on Apr 29?
  4. Is the correct interpretation that HANA deletes complete backup generations based on the scheduler run and generation eligibility, not simply based on the file date?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Question Regarding Authorization Requirement for Finance Plan Import (BUKRS_GLRLDNR_CATEGORY_P)
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We encountered the following authorization error when the customer attempted to import finance plan data:

Error Message: FINS_ACDOCP_PLANNING056
You do not have authorization to save planning data for source xx, category Z_FI_TAG00, controlling area Axxx, company code 00xx.
Please maintain authorization object K_ACDOCP_P.

As a workaround, we resolved the issue by changing the restriction of the customer's business role "BR_CORP_ADV" for:

Company Code / Ledger / Category (BUKRS_GLRLDNR_CATEGORY_P)
→ Set the entire restriction to Unrestricted (*) for all values

After setting the restriction type to unrestricted for all Company Code / Ledger / Category values, the finance plan import worked successfully.

Question

Does anyone know since when this unrestricted setting for all values of BUKRS_GLRLDNR_CATEGORY_P became required in order to import finance plan data?

Specifically:

  • Was this requirement introduced/changed in a particular version upgrade?
  • Or has this been standard SAP behavior for a long time?

We found a similar KBA dated 2025/11/18, so it appears this may not be related to 2602VUP.
However, we would like to understand when/why this authorization setup became necessary.

Any insight would be appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: HANA Cockpit retention not deleting old SYSTEMDB back...
entry.content:
Hi @FPT ,

Thank you for this very well structured question summary.

I think the HANA Cockpit is functioning as expected.
Please note that the "backup generations" in HANA Cockpit differ from the number of data backup files.
Please refer to SAP Note 2852302 - Backup Retention Policy is not Working as Expected, which provides detailed information on how the retention policy works.
You can find the note here: https://me.sap.com/notes/2852302.

Additional remark, if you check the data backups between April 29, 21:00 PM and April 30, 02:00 AM, I think you will likely find only two data backups remaining:
---------------------------------------
2026-04-28 02:00
2026-04-29 02:00
---------------------------------------

Best regards,
Charles

entry.title: My transport to BW UAT is failing even after trying all the solutions.
entry.content:
I added a field to 0EQUIPMENT and marked it as a Navigational attribute. This 0EQUIPMENT is directly used in composite provider so the flag Usable as Info provider is checked, and I haven't made any changes in the checkboxes. 

Everything was OK in DEV but while importing it in QB1 (UAT), I am getting the error: Characteristic 0EQUIPMENT is disabled as an InfoProvider, but HCPR exists

Message No. R7B351

Diagnosis

Characteristic 0EQUIPMENT is used in other objects (in a SAP HANA CompositeProvider for example). Characteristic 0EQUIPMENT must therefore be flagged as an InfoProvider.

 

Procedure

If you want to disable the InfoProvider setting, delete characteristic 0EQUIPMENT from the objects where it is used.

. I have tried all the solutions, but nothing is working. It is needed the flag to be checked.

entry.title: Re: SAP ByDesign ManageEmailActivityIn
entry.content:
Hello Lewis,

I am not a PDI expert, but I'd like to share something about the standard function on sales order output in ByD system. You may check if the standard function fill your request. 

In sales orders, you  can manually click 'submit - send order confirmation to account' button. System then generates the output document (sales order confirmation) and sends it to the account. In case mail is set as the output channel, a mail with the sales order confirmation PDF will be sent out. 

In case, you are using web service to release the sales order, you can set the auto-send when the order is released. see details in the KBA 2914079 (https://me.sap.com/notes/0002914079).

Hope the info is helpful.

Thanks,

Adie

entry.title: AI in Healthcare: Inviting Ideas, Challenges, and Perspectives
entry.content:
Reimagining Healthcare with AI: A Call for Collaboration

Healthcare is going through a significant shift today. With increasing data availability and rapid advancements in Artificial Intelligence (AI), there’s a growing opportunity to rethink how healthcare systems operate - from day-to-day processes to long-term decision-making.

As part of the CIS Innovation Incubation team, we’ve been exploring how AI can be applied in practical ways to improve efficiency, support better decisions, and create meaningful value for healthcare providers and stakeholders.

Why Healthcare?

Healthcare plays a critical role in society, covering areas like patient care, operations, supply chain, compliance, and planning. At the same time, many of these processes still depend heavily on manual effort, disconnected systems, and delayed insights.

There’s already a large amount of data available across healthcare systems, but it is often underutilised. This creates an opportunity to use AI more effectively.

Some areas where AI could help include:

  • Making sense of large and complex datasets
  • Reducing repetitive and manual tasks
  • Supporting faster and more accurate decision-making
  • Enabling more proactive and predictive operations

What We’re Trying to Do

Our goal is to better understand real-world challenges and explore where AI can genuinely add value.

We are focusing on:

  • Identifying practical pain points in healthcare processes
  • Understanding challenges faced by customers and partners
  • Exploring ideas collaboratively instead of building in isolation

Where AI Could Potentially Help

Without going into specific solutions, some broad areas where AI seems promising include:

  • Analysing data to generate quicker and more useful insights
  • Improving planning and resource utilisation
  • Automating document-heavy or repetitive workflows
  • Making information easier to access through conversational tools
  • Monitoring processes in real time to identify risks early

How We’re Approaching This

We’re taking a collaborative and iterative approach:

  • Engaging with Product Managers and healthcare teams
  • Having discussions to understand real challenges
  • Exploring ideas through quick prototypes where possible

The focus is on staying practical and grounded in real needs.

Call for Inputs

If you’re working in healthcare, your perspective would be really valuable:

  • What are the key challenges you face today?
  • Which processes are the most time-consuming or error-prone?
  • Where do you see real potential for AI adoption?

Your inputs will help us focus on solving the right problems.

What’s Next

Based on the feedback we receive, we’ll:

  • Identify high-impact areas
  • Build initial prototypes
  • Iterate with stakeholders

Conclusion

AI has strong potential to improve how healthcare systems operate, but the real value lies in solving meaningful problems. Collaboration will be key to making that happen.

Looking forward to hearing your thoughts.

entry.title: Norway VAT ID (NO1) not printed on invoice despite correct tax determination
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

we are currently facing an issue with VAT ID printing for Norway and would appreciate your support.

Problem description:
A Norwegian VAT ID (format: NO1) is maintained in the customer master data, but it is not printed on the invoice, although everything seems to be correctly configured.

Details:

  • Ship-to country/region: Norway (NO)
  • Tax departure country/region: Germany (DE)
  • Tax code: A7
  • Tax determination: Working correctly

We also observed that this issue can occur when the country of the VAT ID does not match the customer's country. However, in this case, there is no mismatch, so this should not be the root cause.

Question:
What could be the reason that the Norwegian VAT ID is not printed on the invoice despite correct master data and tax determination? Are there specific configuration settings, forms, or routines that control this behavior for Norway?

Any hints or guidance would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

Kathi

entry.title: Re: Use other external SMS/Email providers
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP’s response is correct — out-of-the-box, CDC primarily supports listed SMS/email providers, and anything outside that typically requires using Extensions or custom integration logic.

Just to add from implementation experience:
If you’re planning to use a provider like Odigo (or any non-listed provider), the usual approach is:

  • Use CDC Extensions / Webhooks to trigger an external API call
  • Handle OTP/TFA logic outside CDC (your backend or middleware)
  • Return status back to CDC flow for verification

This gives you full flexibility in choosing regional or specialized providers, especially if you need better delivery performance in specific markets.

One thing to keep in mind — when working with external SMS providers, make sure they support:

  • Reliable DLR (delivery reports)
  • Low-latency routing for OTP use cases
  • Failover/fallback (important for authentication flows)

We’ve seen this approach work well particularly in regions where default providers don’t perform consistently.

If helpful, there are providers that focus specifically on high-delivery routing in emerging markets (Africa, Middle East, etc.), which can be integrated via API in this same extension-based setup.

Hope this helps clarify

entry.title: SAP Settlement Management – Retrieve PDF Form via API for Lobster Integration (XInvoice/ZUGFeRD)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm facing an integration challenge in the area of SAP Settlement Management (Condition Contract Management) and hope someone has had similar experiences or can point me in the right direction.

Background:

My customer uses Lobster as their EDI/middleware solution. For standard billing, the integration is already working: Lobster retrieves the invoice PDF via the standard OData API:

 
 
GET /sap/opu/odata/SAP/API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV/GetPDF

This works smoothly and is well documented.

The Problem:

Now we need to build an analogous process for the Customer Rebate Document from Settlement Management – i.e., the document issued to the customer during bonus settlement (Customer Rebate Settlement Document / credit memo equivalent).

The goal is to also retrieve this form via an interface and pass it to Lobster, so it can be further processed and sent as an XInvoice (XRechnung) or ZUGFeRD-compliant electronic invoice.

My Research So Far:

I have searched extensively but have not found a comparable standard API:

  • On the SAP API Business Hub (api.sap.com), there does not appear to be a dedicated OData API for Settlement Management forms / Condition Contract documents
  • API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV only covers classic SD billing documents, not Settlement Management documents
  • I am not aware of any RFC-enabled function modules that cover this use case

My Questions for the Community:

  1. Is there a (perhaps lesser-known) OData or REST API in the SAP standard that allows programmatic retrieval of a Settlement Management form PDF?
  2. Would an RFC/BAPI approach be feasible?
  3. Are there alternative integration approaches, such as:
    • Direct integration via SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) / Integration Suite?
    • Output via SPAD/Spool with subsequent retrieval via web service?
    • Using Smart Forms / Adobe Forms Output Management with a downstream retrieval step?
  4. Has anyone successfully implemented this process – rebate settlement document → middleware → XInvoice/ZUGFeRD delivery – and can share their approach?

System Environment: SAP S/4HANA (On-Premise), Lobster as middleware, target format XRechnung / ZUGFeRD 2.x

Any hint is appreciated – even a confirmed "this is not possible in standard" would already be helpful and move us forward. Thanks in advance!

Best regards

entry.title: Electronic Bank Statement Process in SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
Introduction

This blogs aims to explore the importance of Bank Reconciliation process within the SAP S4 HANA system. I have tried to cover the key features, functionalities, implementation considerations and challenges that have experience in my implementation journey.

Importance of Bank Reconciliation

Bank statements are very critical for any firm as they give them a clear understanding of their receivables and payables. This also helps them to understand their accurate cash balances to make any informed decisions.

Bank Reconciliation process helps the firm to understand any discrepancies that might arise due to the few of the following reasons below.

  1. Timing of the transactions
  2. Unknown transactions
  3. Payments received but not cleared

Understanding Electronic Bank Statement Reconciliation Process

EBS Process

 External bank statements are imported into S/4HANA (e.g. CAMT.053) and processed via batch job (FF_5 or Import Bank Statement App).

Once the statement is imported in the system, it attempts automatic matching bank statement items to accounting postings i.e. open items on the bank G/L, customer/vendor clears, payments received/paid. Unmatched items are reviewed and handled in the Post‑Processing Worklist (manual reconciliation, additional postings for bank charges, interest, FX differences or adjustments).

 Matched items are cleared in the bank G/L (reconciliation) account; after reconciliation and any necessary adjustments, the balance of the Main bank G/L will equal the reconciled bank statement balance.

Key Features of Bank Reconciliation in S4 HANA

  • Automated bank‑statement processing: S/4HANA imports statements in common formats (MT940, BAI2, CAMT.053, etc.) and posts them automatically, cutting down manual data entry and intervention.
  • Real‑time posting and reporting: Reconciliations and postings update immediately, giving an accurate, up‑to‑date view of cash positions.
  • Exception handling and manual clearing: Items that cannot be matched automatically are flagged for review and cleared or posted manually.
  • Audit trail and compliance: All reconciliation actions are logged, providing a clear audit trail and supporting regulatory compliance.
  • Improved user experience: SAP Fiorio apps gives enhance features to process and clear the bank statement items (Reprocess bank statement item app, Manage reprocessing rules etc)

Electronic Bank Statement Process Flow

 

 

 

 Key Steps in Bank Reconciliation 

  • Verify Completeness of Imported Bank Statements: Verify that the imported Electronic Bank Statements are error-free and complete.
  • Post bank statements are imported into SAP S4 system: With Electronic Bank Statements (EBS) enabled and imported daily, Bank Reconciliation Analysts can generate reports to compare bank and book balances as often as needed. To facilitate this process, the following Fiori applications can be used to compare book-bank balances.

    Reprocess Bank Statement items: This app provides comprehensive details of bank statement items, along with their posting and processing status. It enables business users to Identify transactions that have not been fully posted yet (Colored in Yellow and Red).

    Additionally, the user can filter the data based on different parameters

    1. Notes to Payee information
    2. BTC Code (Identifies the nature of transaction).

     

  • Clearing of Cash and Cash Equivalent G/L account Open items

  • There are several open item managed G/L accounts that are established as clearing accounts to facilitate the automatic posting of bank statement items through Electronic Bank Statements.

    Typically, clearing accounts are processed automatically when bank statements are imported. However, due to insufficient data to automate the process, some clearing accounts must be cleared manually.

    Below are the 3 steps in which clearing takes place:

    1. Clearing with EBS: Standard EBS functionality offers automatic clearing of clearing accounts, if it meets the specified clearing criteria.
    2. Automatic Clearing Program (F.13): For certain clearing accounts where the EBS automatic clearing does not apply, the Automatic Clearing Program can be utilized.
    3. Manual Clearing: If the automatic clearing criteria are not met, then the clearing of open items will be handled manually.

    Below are some benefits the organization received by implementing EBS.

     

    • Faster Month-End Close: Automation of bank statement helped to reduce the month-end close as the jobs are executed daily giving more time for the analyst to identify the issues and resolve on a daily basis.
    • Improved Accuracy and Compliance: Automation minimizes manual intervention, ensuring more accurate financial statements. Every processing step is logged, supporting audit and compliance requirements.
    • Real time visibility: Electronic bank statements get processed real-time, giving true update on company’s cash position.
    • Manage Reprocessing Rules: Reconciliation Analyst can create rules based on identical bank statements items that similar and follow the same posting rules using S/4 Fiori app.

     

     

Challenges & Lessons learned from my project:

 

Bank statement does not include unique external transaction (BTC or BAI Code) for Bank-to-Bank Account Transfers and update trading partner.

 Solution: Leverage unique identifier from the Memo Line and configure a search string to post to the desired posting rule. Use search string to update the trading partner using BDC field and value fields.

 Foreign payments – Exchange rate difference was being posted to DSA account rather FX Gain/loss Account.

 Solution: Use substitution rule to post the difference between bank and book balances to FX gain/loss account. Setup tolerance group for user and G/L account.

 Payment Document number as string was sent in bank statement under End to End id tag instead of Payment reference which did not allow to auto-clear outgoing clearing account. 

Solution: Use the search string to extract Payment document number from the string and assign interpretation algorithm 020-Doc number search to auto-clear the outgoing clearing account.

 Conclusion

In SAP S/4HANA, bank reconciliation is enhanced through automation, real-time processing, and seamless integration with other modules. These advancements reduce manual effort, minimize errors, and provide instant visibility into cash positions and exceptions. Ultimately, effective bank reconciliation fosters financial transparency, strengthens internal controls, and accelerates the financial close, making it a strategic enabler for modern finance teams.

entry.title: Does S/4HANA Really Support 10 Currencies? Fact vs. Myth
entry.content:
Applicability: S/4HANA On-Premise and Private Cloud
Read Time: 4 Minutes

SAP S/4HANA Finance FIN General Ledger SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition  SAP S/4HANA 

Recently, I was working with a multinational client who had complex multi-currency requirements. They needed to track:

We often hear the claim that S/4HANA supports up to 10 currencies, a massive jump from the limits of ECC. But as I worked through the implementation, I realized it is vital to distinguish between what is "supported" and what is "fully integrated."

The Myth: "All 10 Currencies Are Created Equal"

While it is a fact that the Universal Journal (ACDOCA) has fields for up to 10 currencies (WSL, TSL, HSL, KSL, and OSL through GSL), it is a myth that they all behave the same way in every process.

The technical reality is that the BSEG table has not been extended. It still only holds a maximum of 3 parallel currencies. These are your 1st, 2nd, and 3rd FI Currencies configured in transaction FINSC_LEDGER.

Why the "BSEG Limit" Still Matters

You might wonder: if we have ACDOCA, why care about the old BSEG table?

The answer lies in legacy processes and historical data. Many programs still rely on BSEG logic. Unless you have Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA) activated (available since S/4HANA 2022), currencies beyond the first three—those not integrated with BSEG—face certain limitations:

  • Historical Rate Gaps: Processes like Fixed Asset Accounting (specifically Asset Under Construction settlements) and Material Ledger often require historical exchange rates.
  • The Fallback Effect: For the 4th currency and beyond (that is the non-integrated BSEG currencies), the system may not have the historical data stored. In these cases, it falls back to the current exchange rate.
  • Rounding Differences: This fallback can lead to differences caused by rounding or varying exchange rates that wouldn't exist in the BSEG-integrated currencies.

The Strategy: Choosing Your "BSEG Relevant" Currencies

Because only 3 currencies can be integrated with BSEG, you must choose them wisely. A key rule to remember is that currency types in the customer namespace (Z) cannot be BSEG-relevant*. You must use standard SAP currency types.

One possible approach  was:
To ensure maximum integration and accuracy for their IFRS and Group needs, we carefully mapped their three BSEG slots:

  1. 1st Currency: Company Code Currency (10)
  2. 2nd Currency: Group/Global Currency (30)
  3. 3rd Currency: Hard Currency (40)

We chose Hard Currency (40) to meet the EUR reporting requirement. While Hard Currency is typically intended for high-inflation regions, it was the most appropriate standard currency type, in our scenario, to ensure BSEG integration and avoid the "fallback" issues associated with freely defined currencies.

!! Note:
In this particular scenario, we selected Currency Type 40 (Hard Currency) for our third BSEG slot to meet EUR reporting needs. However, this should not be taken as a universal rule. Every client environment is different.

When Should You Use the Other 7 Currencies?

In my opinion, if your goal is primarily Reporting, the 4th through 10th currencies are excellent. They are supported in:

  • Fiori Apps (like Journal Entry Analyzer and Audit Journal)
  • G/L Account Line-Item reports (FAGLL03, FAGLL03H)
  • CDS-based Analytics

Conclusion: S/4HANA does indeed provide 10 currency fields, but the "10-currency" claim is only a partial truth for those not using UPA. For deep financial processes, the "Power of 3" still rules.

Important Disclaimer: The configuration choices mentioned above were specific to a particular business case. A thorough impact analysis of your unique business requirements are required before making any currency configuration suggestions.

entry.title: Re: Problem: How to make a custom field non-editable when editing a sales order
entry.content:
Hi Slanzetta,

if you added your custom field via the Custom Fields or Custom Fields and Logic app, I would first suggest checking the configuration there.

Open the app, search for your field, and navigate to the User Interface tab. Then select the relevant UI (via the arrow on the right). In some cases, SAP already provides out-of-the-box functionality to mark the field as read-only for a specific UI.

If this option is not available, you can alternatively implement a BDEF extension for the corresponding (extended) CDS projection view entity. For example:

extension for projection;
extend behavior for C_YOURCONSUMEVIEWENTITY
{
  field ( readonly )
    ZZ1_SettCont_SDH;
}

Best regards

entry.title: Re: SAP Make-to-Order Policy: When entering the manufactu...
entry.content:
Good day @shiwa 

Thank you for your question.

The scenario you described is not only a production version issue. How the Public Cloud handles make-to-order planning and individual customer segments matters here. See KBA 3485915 on MTO and ETO scenarios and its area of performance. In a nutshell, it is not possible to plan individual customer segments in a separate MRP area. Consult Note 550844 for further details.

So, in your case, the behaviour is expected if your process depends on storage location level planning or MRP area logic for a make-to-order requirement. What this basically means is that:

  • Strategy 20 creates individual customer requirements.
  • These individual customer requirements are planned in the plant MRP area.
  • The storage location you entered in the sales order item does not make the individual customer segment planned in a storage location MRP area.
  • Storage location MRP logic is not the right design for MTO individual segments.
  • You should not use the production version’s goods receipt storage location as the mechanism to drive component issue storage locations for individual customer segments.
  • If no production version is selected, make sure you check whether the selection criteria and master data are still valid at plant level, not at the areal level of the storage location MRP.

See the following reference for component storage location requirement and production version.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/59acce5314894208e10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/c234b8535c39b44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

So, to answer your specific requirement, if the what you need is that component issue storage location should be proposed automatically based on individual customer segment requirements, then there is no standard way to achieve this by using storage location MRP areas in an MTO strategy 20 scenario.

I recommend you separate the two topics:

  • Option 1 is to use plant level MTO planning and standard component storage location determination. Keep the MTO planning at plant level. Then use the supported BOM explosion logic to maintain component storage location determination.
  • Option 2 is to use the production version to determine the BOM and routing, and the receipt storage location for the produced material. Do not use it as the main driver for component issue storage locations.

If your customer requirement strictly depends on storage location level MTO planning, the process design should be reviewed. The closest standard approach is to use plant level MTO planning and determine the component issue storage location through the supported options.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: 1H2026 Release: A journey to Autonomous Payroll with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:
Hello Everyone!

To advance our vision of an autonomous payroll, we are introducing four specialized Payroll Agents for the SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) solution in this first half 2026 release.

You can now benefit from automations across 1) payroll and time explanations, 2) alert resolution, 3) rule creation, and 4) data integration. These AI agents detect, explain, and resolve issues before they impact your workforce. They streamline how payroll teams prepare and run payroll by orchestrating checks across time, workflow, and payroll controls.

Below are the key highlights, but be sure to explore the What's New Viewer for the complete release notes and full details on everything we’re delivering.

Payroll Explanation Agent

With this update, the Payroll Explanation Agent connects Payroll and Time sheet data into one unified explanation, giving employees a clear, easy‑to‑understand view of their pay statements and the time data behind them. Employees get crisp, clear and fast answers without relying on HR. This enhances employee experience, builds trust and confidence in payroll accuracy and lowers operational costs. PostNL, one of our customers based in Netherlands, expects 80% cost reduction in handling payroll questions with an AI agent. Additionally, support now extends to Brazil and Canada, building on the 2025 release in the USA, UK, Australia, Mexico, the Netherlands, and Germany.

  • See the Payroll Explanation Agent in action here (video)

 

[Screenshot] Payroll Explanation Agent now integrates Time sheet data and supports Brazil and Canada

Payroll Alert Resolution Agent

This agent streamlines how payroll administrators manage Payroll Control Center (PCC) alerts by turning complex investigations into a guided, intelligent workflow. It explains why alerts occur, identifies the most likely root causes, and recommends prioritized fixes directly within the payroll control center. Administrators can also create new validation rules using natural‑language through a conversational interface.

  • See the Payroll Alert Resolution Agent in action here (video)

[Screenshot] Payroll Alert Resolution Agent in ECP

Payroll Validation Rule Agent

This rule creation agent introduces a conversational, AI‑assisted way for payroll administrators to build new validation rules directly within the Payroll Control Center. Instead of navigating technical configuration steps, admins simply describe the rule they need in natural language.

Instead of navigating technical configuration steps, admins can simply describe the rule they need in natural language making the whole process of validation rule creation 50% faster.

[Screenshot] Payroll Validation Rule Agent in ECP

Employee Data Integration Agent

The agent helps payroll administrator resolve payroll replication errors by translating complex technical messages into clear, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central–focused guidance. It identifies impacted EC fields based on ECP mappings and provides step‑by‑step recommendations for fixing data issues. This release addresses manual corrections.

For example, a missing cost center for an employee. Instead of leaving the admin to interpret a technical error message, the agent analyzes organizational data and suggests the most likely cost center—based on where the employee’s peers in the same department and reporting to the same manager are assigned.

The result: cleaner data, fewer payroll interruptions, and far less time spent resolving issues after the fact.

  • See the Employee Data Integration Agent in action here (video)

[Screenshot] Employee Data Integration Agent in ECP

 

What Else is New this Release?

There are a number of additional innovations available for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll in the first half 2026 release, including

  • Enhancements to Payroll Control Center: Manage Payroll Activities
  • Enhanced Gender Field Replication from Employee Central to Employee Central Payroll
  • Dozens of legal changes for specific country/region (documented in detail in the Regulatory Change Manager)
  • And more

See the What's New Viewer for more information on these features.

Where Can I Find More Information?

Here are additional resources for those interested in learning more about SAP Payroll solutions

  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Website
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll SAP Help (Implementation) (Requires ID)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Community Page (Discussion Forum) (Requires ID)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Learning Journey (Educational Content) (Requires SAP Lea...
  • Other recent release highlights for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
    • 2H/2025 Release Highlight Blog
    • 1H/2025 Release Highlight Blog
    • 2H/2024 Release Highlight Blog
    • 1H/2024 Release Highlight Blog

For information on release highlights across SAP SuccessFactors HCM, visit this one stop shop for all product release and road map information

Show existing fields using UI Adaptation

entry.title: Re: CLM Lease-out revenue postings on cost center / wbs-e...
entry.content:
Hi @Chris1973 ,

Thanks for your very extensive answer. Unfortunately, it is not the answer we hope to receive. I am aware that we can use cost centers and wbs-elements. Both are meant to post expenses and not revenues.

One company code of ours is leasing out real estate, meaning this is the revenue driver. It would be strange to post it as negative costs on a cost center or create a project structure for these postings. I understand the reasoning from a lease-in point of view, but not really for lease-out. If it was a lease-out beneath a lease-in it would also make sense.

Looking back to RE-FX the contract and master data objects where the cost objects. Meaning that we were able to create a P&L on these objects. In CLM the master data objects have disappeared or, in case of usable/rentable objects, also assigned to a cost center / wbs-element. In the past RE-FX was not linked to the SD functionality or profitability analysis. However, then we could use the RE-FX objects/contracts.

If we go for revenue postings on the profit center, we must use a dummy cost center. This does not feel correct. We cannot use an actual revenue cost element, but the GL account must still be marked as cost element.

We are searching for a future proof solution where we can use the lease-out contracts to generate revenue and post these on revenue elements with a correct assignment for managerial reporting. It would make sense to move towards Profitability Analysis because we want to use additional dimensions as well.

I hope that SAP will also read this thread, but perhaps I will create an influence request for it.

Best regards, Jeroen

entry.title: Enhancing Enterprise Applications: AI-powered User Experience with SAP CAP AI Plugin
entry.content:
In today’s world, users expect their applications to smartly reduce their manual effort and help them complete their tasks quickly.

And enterprise applications are no exception. Imagine looking at a huge, blank form that you must fill out to complete a mundane but mandatory task at the office. You can practically feel the energy drain out of your body as the form stares back at you. Suddenly, a coffee break looks enticing 🙂

But what if your application could generate recommendations based on the data available in the system, and dramatically cut your effort to just eyeballing the correctness of most of the fields, only requiring you to fill in a few?

While this may be on every user’s wish list, implementing these AI-driven smart features in existing (or even new) applications can require a lot of development effort.

At SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP), we believe in reducing boilerplate code and letting developers focus on intent. Hence, in the April release of CAP, we have released the SAP CAP AI plugin for node.js. (Watch this space for the Java version, which will be released soon).

This plugin will enable existing and new CAP applications to use recommendations generated by the RPT-1 Generative AI model provided by SAP AI Core. The recommendations will then be pre-filled in the UI of the application. All this is out-of-the-box, without writing a single line of code.

The CAP AI plugin does three main things:

  1. Simplifies AI Core configuration
  2. Provide recommendations on the UI coming from RPT-1
  3. Inherits the best of CAP

Simplifying AI Core configuration –

Admin tasks such as creating a new SAP AI Core resource group per tenant and creating an RPT-1 deployment are automated using the “AICore” service created by the plugin.

Recommendations in the UI application –

Any entity with a value help (@Common.ValueList on the property or @CDS.odata.valuelist on the association target) will be used by the plugin to show the recommendations.

 

 

 

 

 

 

The best of CAP

All the battle-tested enterprise readiness of CAP is inherently part of the plugin. It also supports multi-tenancy, and local testing without an SAP AI Core instance.

You can read more about the plugin here - https://github.com/cap-js/ai

We are excited to have you try out this plugin.  If you have any feature requests or suggestions, please feel free to reach out to us.

Till then, happy coding!

entry.title: Re: ECC Transaction/Fiori app to be used for Mass deletio...
entry.content:
Hello, 

As observed, requisition items cannot be set for deletion in the mass changes app. Also, deletion indicator is not available in MEMASSRQ. In order to set the EBAN-LOEKZ flag, please use BAPI_PR_CHANGE calling it for all requisitions you need to change. This is documented in the following KBA:

2666219 - Deletion flag indicator can not be used with MEMASSRQ - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA.

Regards, 

 

Maria

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: BTP User Cannot Receive “Forgot Password” Email After...
entry.content:
Hi James,

Adding a user in the Users section of the BTP Cockpit does not actually create a new user. It is just an entry mapping 'some user' with their roles. The actual user creation (and password maintenance) happens in the Identity Provider linked to your BTP sub account (see Trust Configuration). In case you are using the 'Default Identity Provider' (this would use your S-user or P-user created by SAP) try and log on the IdP directly via https://accounts.sap.com/ui/protected/profilemanagement. In case you are using a custom Identity Authentication Service (IAS) tenant, first create a user in the IAS via https://your-tenant-id.accounts.ondemand.com/admin and then the mapping in the BTP Cockpit will take effect.

entry.title: Beyond the Bot: Why Utilities Need “Agentic” AI on SAP BTP to Solve the Grid Complexity Crisis
entry.content:
Beyond the Bot: Why Utilities Need “Agentic” AI on SAP BTP to Solve the Grid Complexity Crisis

 

The Grid Complexity Wall

The utility industry is hitting a wall—and it’s not a small one.

For decades, power grids were predictable. Energy flowed in one direction: from generation to transmission to distribution to the customer. Systems like SAP IS-U were designed around this linear model. Billing cycles were stable, meter roads were periodic, and exceptions were manageable.

That world no longer exists.

Now things change, today’s grid is dynamic, decentralized, and highly unpredictable. Electric vehicles charge at random times. Rooftop solar pushes power back into the grid. Heat pumps create new consumption patterns. Customers are no longer just consumers, they are producers.

This shift has introduced a level of complexity that traditional systems were never designed to handle.

Due to this now?

Utilities are now overwhelmed with:

  • Billing exceptions.
  • Meter data inconsistencies.
  • Unexpected usage of spikes.
  • Data reconciliation issues across systems .

 

Many organizations tried to solve this with automation, especially RPA. But here’s the reality: bots don’t solve complexity. They only automate repetition.

 

A bot can follow a script. It cannot understand context, and that’s exactly where the problem lies.

To manage the “messy grid” of today, utilities need to move beyond deterministic automation. They need systems that can interpret, decide, and act.

This is where Agentic AI comes in.

 

Why a “Messy Core” Kills “AI”

Before we talk about AI, we need to talk about architecture. One of the biggest misconceptions is that you can simply “add AI” on top of an existing SAP landscape. AI is only as effective as the environment it operates in.

Agentic AI systems depend on something critical: observability.

They need to understand:

  • What rules exist.
  • How decisions are made.
  • Where do the data come from.
  • What events are happening in real time.

If your core SAP system is filled with deeply embedded custom logic—user exits, enhancements, Z-programs—then that logic is invisible to AI.

 

It becomes a black box and if AI cannot “see” the rules, it cannot reason.

This is why Clean Core is not just an SAP best practice anymore—it is a foundational requirement for AI.

A clean core ensures:

  • Business logic is transparent.
  • Processes are standardized.
  • Extensions are externalized and visible.

 

This is where SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) plays a key role. A simple way to think about it:

  • S/4HANA is the system that executes transactions reliably .
  • BTP is the layer where intelligence, decisions, and adaptability live

If the core is the engine, BTP is the brain. Without that separation, AI cannot function effectively.

Architecture View: Clean Core + Agentic AI on BTP

 

 

Agentic AI vs. Traditional RPA

Let’s make this real with a common utility scenario.

Scenario: High Usage Billing Dispute

A customer receives a bill that is significantly higher than expected and raises a dispute.

What happened today with RPA?

  • The system flags the anomaly
  • A bot may stop the invoice or trigger a workflow
  • The case is routed to a human agent
  • Investigation is manual, time-consuming, and inconsistent

The bot did its job—but only partially. It identified the issue but could not resolve it.

Now imagine the same scenario with Agentic AI

An AI agent doesn’t just flag—it investigates.

It can:

  • Analyze historical consumption patterns
  • Cross-check weather data for abnormal usage
  • Validate meter health from AMI systems
  • Review distributed energy resource activity (like solar export)
  • Identify whether the issue is behavioral, environmental, or technical

Then it can act:

  • Determine probable root cause
  • Trigger a field service request if needed
  • Adjust billing or flag for human approval
  • Log the entire decision process for audit

The key difference is simple:

Bots automate steps.
Agents handle outcomes.

This shift—from execution to reasoning—is what makes Agentic AI fundamentally different.

The Roadmap to Autonomous Utility

Moving to this model doesn’t happen overnight. But there is a clear path forward.

  1. Event-Driven Foundations

Traditional SAP systems operate in batches. That doesn’t work in real-time grid.

Utilities need to adopt event-driven architecture using services like SAP Event Mesh.

This allows systems to:

  • React instantly to meter events
  • Trigger workflows in real time
  • Enable AI agents to act as events occur

Without events, there is no real-time intelligence.

  1. Externalized Logic

One of the biggest shifts is moving business logic out of the core.

This includes:

  • Billing rules
  • Validation logic
  • Regulatory checks
  • Exception handling

Using CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) on BTP, this logic becomes:

  • Modular
  • Transparent
  • Easier to maintain
  • Accessible to AI systems

This is critical because AI needs structured, visible logic to operate effectively.

  1. Semantic Data Layer

AI is only as good as the data it uses.

Utilities typically have fragmented data across:

  • SAP systems
  • GIS platforms
  • AMI systems
  • Customer platforms

A semantic layer helps unify this data into a consistent model.

This ensures:

  • One version of truth
  • Better context for AI decisions
  • Reduced ambiguity

Without this, even the most advanced AI will produce unreliable results.

Governance: The Guardrails of Agentic AI

With great intelligence comes great responsibility, especially in a regulated industry like utilities.

You cannot allow AI to operate without control.

Key concerns include:

  • Regulatory compliance
  • Billing accuracy
  • Auditability
  • Customer trust

The answer is not to limit AI—but to govern it properly.

This includes:

  • Human-in-the-loop checkpoints for critical decisions
  • Clear approval workflows
  • Full traceability of AI decisions
  • Strong grounding using SAP AI Core

Governance ensures that AI operates within defined boundaries.

In fact, Clean Core governance becomes even more important in an AI-driven world. It ensures that all decisions—whether human or machine—follow a consistent and compliant path.

Conclusion: Survival of the Modern Grid

Utilities today are at a turning point.

The complexity of the grid is not going away. It will only increase.

Organizations that continue to rely on heavy core customization are creating a long-term problem. They are building systems that are harder to adapt, harder to scale, and impossible for AI to understand.

In many ways, this becomes a “digital anchor”—holding them back while the industry moves forward.

On the other hand, utilities that embrace:

  • Clean Core principles
  • BTP as an innovation platform
  • Event-driven architecture
  • Agentic AI

will be in a position to not just manage complexity—but to turn it into an advantage.

The journey doesn’t need to start big.

Start small.

Pick one high-volume exception process—billing disputes, meter validation, or service orders. Externalize the logic. Build it on BTP. Introduce intelligence step by step.

That’s how transformation begins.

Because the future utility is not just automated.

It is autonomous, adaptive, and intelligent.

And that future starts now.

entry.title: Re: Custom Fields Failing to Influence Procurement Pricin...
entry.content:
Good day @alan_tsai 

Thank you for your question.

The thing to note is that you are creating custom fields on the PO item and maintaining condition records with those custom fields doe not automatically make those fields available in the procurement pricing communication structure.

There is no configuration path in the system where you can manually map PO item custom fields to KOMP or to a procurement pricing communication entity. KBA 3687197 that limitation.

This is why you see the condition type PPR0 in pricing analysis, but the custom field values appear as initial or Not Found. The condition access can technically exist, but the pricing engine does not receive the custom field value in the pricing communication layer. Therefore, the system cannot use that custom field value to determine the correct condition record. This basically means:

  • The custom fields can exist on the PO item.
  • The custom fields can be visible in the PO UI if you enable it correctly.
  • The condition type PPR0 can still be processed.
  • However, the custom PO item fields are not automatically passed into procurement pricing comms.
  • There is no released config activity to map those fields manually to KOMP.
  • The BAdI MM_PUR_S4_PO_DEFINE_CNDNCTRL can trigger or control pricing determination, but it does not create a missing KOMP mapping by itself.
  • If the pricing analysis shows the custom fields as initial, the condition record cannot be found based on those fields.

See KBA 3580006 for details on enhancement option for purchase oder.

So to summarise:

  • In the standard released scope, the PO item custom fields cannot be enabled in the way you are trying to use them.
  • Implementing MM_PUR_S4_PO_DEFINE_CNDNCTRL does not solve the missing field value issue by itself.
  • PRCG_DOC_ITEM_CONDITION_AMOUNT can only be used based on the requirement. See KBA 2756560 and KBA 3687197 for more insights.

My recommended approach is not to use unsupported PO item custom fields directly in the PPR0 access sequence. Instead, consider using one of these options:

  1. Use standard pricing fields wherever possible. Make sure you redesign the access sequence around released standard fields. (most appropriate).
  2. Use MM_PUR_S4_PO_DEFINE_CNDNCTRL only to trigger redetermination. The BAdI can help with when pricing runs, not with making the missing field available in KOMP.
  3. Use alternative condition amount calculation only when the values you need are available. If your requirement is not strict condition record determination, but price calculation based on custom logic, review the purchase order price enhancement approach in KBA 3580006:
    1. Create a custom logic for PRCG_DOC_ITEM_CONDITION_AMOUNT.
    2. Create a calculation routing in SSCUI 102430.
    3. Copy or adapt the pricing procedure in SSUI 101095.
    4. Assign the routine to the condition type in the pricing procedure and test the PO pricing result.

This is only valid if the values needed for the calculation are available in the BAdI context. If your PO item custom fields are not available there, this will not resolve the same root issue.

 

You may also consider raising a feature request if the custom fields must be access sequence fields.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: System cannot be deleted
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Kindly know before deleting a on-prem system, you have to close all the open and draft cases under the system to be deleted.

So pls kindly close all those open cases first, then try to delete the systems again per KBA 1843303 - How to delete On-premise systems - SAP for Me.

In case there is no any open cases under the system but the system still cannot be deleted, feel free to contact us by creating a case using component XX-SER-FORME.

Thanks and best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: BTP User Cannot Receive “Forgot Password” Email After...
entry.content:
Forgot password is functionality is from IAS. For this the user should already be created/onboarded in IAS. When you create an IAS tenant, a user with the same email address will be automatically onboarded in IAS. 

Adding a user in subaccount is only creating a shadow user record that can be used for assigning role collections in the subaccount. It does not create a user in connected IAS.

You need to onboard the user in IAS.

entry.title: Re: Add value to Sales Order header Usage dropdown; confi...
entry.content:
Dear @harshad_p,

In Public Cloud the Sales Order header Usage dropdown cannot be extended by customer configuration. There is no SSCUI, CBC configuration activity or customer-maintainable table available to add new values to this standard dropdown

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3330346

Recommended workaround

Use a custom field at Sales Document/Sales Order header level, preferably with a fixed value help/code list, if the business needs an additional classification value. SAP Public Cloud extensibility content confirms that custom fields can be added to sales documents and propagated through related sales/billing scenarios where supported

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/applying-localization-as-a-self-service-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/adding-custom-fields-to-sales-documents

Summary:

The standard Usage dropdown at Sales Order header level is not extendable by configuration in Public Cloud. Use a clean-core custom field/code list instead or raise an SAP influence request

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2963059

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP Community CPH Meetup - 28th May 2026
entry.content:
SAP Community in CPH - SAVE THE DATE!

On the 28th of May, after Sapphire, we will do our quarterly gathering in CPH. The session will be divided in 2 parts:

  1. The first part will contain talks that are very interesting and relevant to our time from some of the talents from our local community. We are also planning a recap of what was announced in SAPPHIRE this year. Stay tuned for the announcement of the speakers!
  2. For the second part, more informal, we will go to a beer garden almost next door for socialising and having a good time.

The talks will be held at NNIT’s offices in Islands Brygge from 15:00 to 17:00. After that, we are planning to move to Bootleggers, where apart from drinks one can also get dinner. Thank you to NNIT for hosting us!

Remember to sign up here so we know how many people are intending to come! 

Save the date in your calendar!

entry.title: Re: reduce Field Length of System Database Field
entry.content:
Hi,

Reducing NumAtCard from 200 to 100 at the database or field level isn’t supported and shouldn’t be done directly in SQL tables. Making such a change can break SAP Business One upgrade compatibility and may also void system support.

Regards

entry.title: Help Keep SAP IBP #1: Your Product Experience Matters
entry.content:
Product Experience Survey - Help Us Make SAP IBP Work Better for You

 When the Product Experience Survey appears in your SAP IBP application, it’s your chance to share what you like, what’s working well and what isn’t. 

We’re looking to understand where you struggle in your daily work—those tasks that take longer than they should or cause friction. We also want to know which features truly make your life easier and work well for you, plus how we can improve your overall experience with SAP IBP applications. Here is a link to a video showing you how to launch the PX Survey in SAP IBP. 

How the PX Survey Works

The PX survey appears directly in your app or can be started manually when you have feedback to share. Your responses are linked to the specific application you’re using, so our development teams get targeted, actionable insights.

 

Where to Find It:

For the Fiori Launchpad you’ll find the survey in the upper right corner using the speech bubble with a smiley icon.

Alternatively you can also use the SAP IBP add-in for Microsoft Excel:

In the IBP Menu goto About -> give Feedback

What’s Included

The survey includes a Product Satisfaction score, Usefulness and Ease of Use ratings, and a comment field for additional details.

 

 

Getting the Most from Your Feedback

Do:

  • Respond when the survey appears—it only takes a moment
  • Focus your comments on the specific app mentioned

Don’t:

  • Include personal information in comments
  • Leave generic responses—specific feedback is more helpful

 

Your Input Makes a Difference

Your feedback helps us understand what’s working and what needs attention. When you take a moment to respond, you’re helping improve the daily experience for yourself and other users.

The next time you see the PX survey, consider sharing your thoughts—it’s one of the most direct ways to influence how SAP IBP evolves.

Reference:

  • Video showing you how to launch the PX Survey in SAP IBP
